metadata.title: Fintech, Banking-as-a-Service and neobanks - the Synapse collapse and the 'FDIC-insured' ledger trap
metadata.frame: Most neobanks are not banks - they ride a handful of small sponsor banks through middleware that keeps the sub-ledger of who owns what. When Synapse failed (2024), ~100,000+ customers were frozen and ~$85-96M went missing despite 'FDIC-insured' marketing, because pass-through insurance covers BANK failure, not a middleware/ledger collapse. A former FDIC Chair as trustee still could not find the money. This block maps the model, the insurance gap, the sponsor-bank consent-order wave, and the 2025 repeat (Solid). Companion to [[macro-uncovered-risk-pools]] and [[macro-bank-htm-marks]].
metadata.relevance: Adds the tech-mediated-deposit node to the uncovered stack; shows 'deposits look insured' is conditional on a private ledger that is the single point of failure; the concentration of fintech programs on a few sponsor banks is a contagion channel back into the FDIC system.
metadata.sources: CNBC/Banking Dive/American Banker/Bloomberg Law (Synapse + trustee McWilliams); FDIC Part 328 + custodial-recordkeeping NPR; OCC/Fed/FDIC July-2024 joint statement; Fed Evolve C&D; CFPB ($46.2M payout); SEC S-1 (Chime); company disclosures; FintechFutures (Solid 2025). Full URLs in the rendered .md.
metadata.note: Synapse shortfall is ~$85-96M (the $65M floor in earlier framing is below all sourced figures); the case was dismissed Nov 2025.

synapse_collapse.summary: A middleware ledger failure that froze 100,000+ users and lost up to ~$96M.

points.detail: Synapse Financial Technologies (Chapter 11, April 2024) sat between fintech apps (Yotta, Juno, Copper) and partner banks (Evolve, AMG, American, Lineage), routing customer money into pooled 'for-benefit-of' (FBO) accounts while keeping the per-customer sub-ledger itself.

>100,000 customers frozen from May 2024; end users were owed ~$265M but partner banks held only ~$180M - a shortfall first reported at ~$85M (trustee Jelena McWilliams, former FDIC Chair, June 2024), later widened to 'up to ~$95M.' Evolve said it had moved >$300M of balances to other partner banks while severing the relationship - a key reconciliation rupture.

The trustee - with subpoena power - still could not determine where the money went; the bankruptcy was DISMISSED Nov 2025, the estate administratively insolvent with no bidders. Reconciliation opacity is the failure mode.

insurance_gap.summary: 'FDIC-insured' was true of the bank and false of the outcome.

points.detail: Pass-through FDIC insurance covers BANK failure only - NOT fintech/middleware failure or a ledger shortfall, and it requires records identifying beneficial owners (which Synapse lacked). No partner bank failed, so insurance never triggered; customers' belief that funds were 'FDIC-insured' did not protect them.

FDIC amended Part 328 (false-advertising/misrepresentation of insured status; compliance Jan 2025) and proposed 'Recordkeeping for Custodial Accounts' (Oct 2024) requiring banks to keep beneficial-owner records for FBO accounts - but as of mid-2026 NO final custodial rule has been published. OCC/Fed/FDIC issued a July-2024 joint statement warning third-party arrangements can mislead consumers about FDIC coverage.
The CFPB allocated ~$46.2M from its Civil Penalty Fund to Synapse/Evolve victims (approved Nov 2025) - roughly half the frozen amount, the first-ever fintech 'bailout' via the CPF.

sponsor_bank_wave.summary: Hundreds of fintech programs ride a few small sponsor banks - and the regulators came for them.

points.detail: At least 8 BaaS sponsor banks hit with FDIC/OCC/Fed orders in 2024: Evolve (Fed C&D, June 2024), Cross River (FDIC, 2023), Blue Ridge (OCC 'troubled condition,' 2024 - exited BaaS, displacing ~70 fintech partners), Lineage, Piermont, Sutton (FDIC, Feb 2024), Thread (FDIC, May 2024). BaaS banks were ~18% of FDIC enforcement actions since Jan 2024.

A single sponsor bank often supports hundreds of fintech programs; one order or BaaS exit cascades to millions of end users (Blue Ridge -> ~70 partners). The pool is small banks carrying tech-scale deposit bases.

neobank_scale.summary: The big names are apps on top of sponsor banks, not banks.

points.detail: Chime (>8.6M active members; IPO June 2025, ~$864M raised) holds deposits at The Bancorp Bank and Stride Bank. Cash App (~57M users) uses Sutton Bank / Wells Fargo pass-through. Dave rides a sponsor bank (FTC sued it Nov 2024). Varo is the ONLY neobank with its own OCC national charter (2020) - and its deposits (~$300M) and losses are deteriorating.
Solid Financial Technologies (BaaS middleware, $330M valuation, >100 fintech programs) filed Chapter 11 in April 2025 - the Synapse pattern again, a year later.

synthesis.summary: Deposits look insured; the private ledger is the single point of failure.

points.detail: BaaS turned 'FDIC-insured' into a marketing claim conditional on accurate beneficial-owner records and a solvent middleware - neither guaranteed. Tech-mediated balances flow across pooled FBO accounts at multiple small sponsor banks via a private sub-ledger; severing one relationship can break reconciliation system-wide, and a former-FDIC-Chair trustee still could not find the money. The same trust-the-ledger defect that this project documents in self-marked balance sheets appears here as literal missing deposits.

non_claims: No claim that pass-through FDIC insurance is invalid - it works when a BANK fails and records are accurate; the gap is middleware/ledger failure.
The Synapse shortfall is ~$85-96M (a range that grew over time); the custodial-recordkeeping rule was NOT finalized as of mid-2026.
Choice Bank / First Fed could not be confirmed as having 2023-2025 BaaS consent orders; the confirmed set is Cross River, Blue Ridge, Lineage, Piermont, Sutton, Thread, Evolve.
Overlay edges are EXCLUDED from the SCC / Z3 / TLA+ proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: pooled for-benefit-of (FBO) custodial deposits + a private sub-ledger; reconciliation failure (Synapse) froze 100,000+ users and lost ~$85-96M; repeated by Solid (2025)
edges.note: OVERLAY: ledger single-point-of-failure; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: pass-through FBO deposits - Chime (Bancorp/Stride), Cash App (Sutton/Wells), Dave; most neobanks are apps on a few small sponsor banks, not banks
edges.note: OVERLAY: fintech-on-bank dependence; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: small FDIC-insured sponsor banks carrying tech-scale deposit bases and hundreds of fintech programs each; ~8 hit with 2024 consent orders, one exit (Blue Ridge) displaced ~70 partners
edges.note: OVERLAY: concentration into the insured system; excluded from proofs

limits: Documents the Synapse collapse (Ch.11 Apr 2024; 100,000+ frozen; owed ~$265M vs ~$180M held = ~$85-96M shortfall; trustee McWilliams; dismissed Nov 2025; CFPB ~$46.2M partial payout); the pass-through-insurance gap (covers bank failure only) and FDIC Part 328 + unfinalized custodial-recordkeeping rule; ~8 sponsor-bank consent orders in 2024 (Evolve/Cross River/Blue Ridge/Lineage/Piermont/Sutton/Thread); neobank scale (Chime/Cash App/Dave ride sponsor banks; Varo the lone chartered one, deteriorating); Solid's 2025 repeat. Overlay edges excluded from proofs.

checks.result: Ch.11 Apr 2024; >100k frozen; owed ~$265M vs ~$180M held; shortfall ~$85M->~$95M; dismissed Nov 2025 (Bloomberg Law/CNBC).
Pass-through covers bank failure only; FDIC Part 328 compliance Jan 2025; custodial-recordkeeping NPR Oct 2024 not finalized; CFPB ~$46.2M (Nov 2025).
Evolve Fed C&D Jun 2024; Blue Ridge OCC Jan 2024 (~70 partners displaced); Cross River/Lineage/Piermont/Sutton/Thread; ~18% of FDIC actions.
Chime IPO Jun 2025 (~$864M); Cash App ~57M; Varo lone charter (~$300M deposits, falling); Solid Ch.11 Apr 2025.
